newsArray.title: CSK vs SRH: Did Chennai Super Kings file a complaint over 'black magic' viral video?
newsArray.description: In a strange turn of events during the IPL 2026 match between Sunrisers Hyderabad (SRH) and Chennai Super Kings (CSK), a peculiar moment caught the attention of fans and has now gone viral. The incident, which many viewers are calling 'black magic,' unfolded in the stands and seemed to coincide with a key wicket that led to CSK's downfall. The bizarre scene occurred in the 17th over of CSK's innings when Shivam Dube, a dangerous hitter known for turning games around was at the crease. With CSK chasing a target of 194, the score stood at 154/7. SRH, desperate to remove Dube, watched as a supporter in the stands appeared to perform a ritual. Holding a lemon, the fan rotated it several times while muttering something under his breath and gesturing toward Dube. Also Read: Not invited to play in final, angry man runs tractor on cricket ground - Watch Just moments later, Dube was dismissed for 21 runs off 16 balls, bowled by Sakib Hussain. The delivery was a full ball, which Dube misjudged and missed completely, sending the ball crashing into the stumps. The dismissal came as a surprise, and many fans took to social media, speculating that the ritual may have had some impact on Dube’s sudden departure. . @ChennaiIPL lodged a complaint with @BCCI https://t.co/kvPTdZNCn1 pic.twitter.com/XsPOAWu9cJ — Maximus Chaturvedi 🪷 (@PsychoSainik) April 19, 2026 The Fake Complaint Controversy In the aftermath of the match, a photoshopped letter claiming that CSK had filed a formal complaint with the BCCI about SRH fans’ alleged involvement in black magic quickly spread across social media. However, this was debunked as fake, with no official complaint being lodged. Despite the controversy, SRH went on to defeat CSK by 10 runs, ending CSK's two-match winning streak and moving up to fourth place on the points table. The incident has left fans intrigued, sparking discussions about superstitions and rituals in the world of cricket.
creator: bibhu prasad ray
